design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse time-period themed platforming levels, collect golden orbs scattered throughout environments, defeat period-specific Drakkon Lord bosses, return orbs to time machine to progress. Repeat across four historical eras before global time deadline expires.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual-axis pressure: navigating challenging platforming mechanics while racing against a 36-minute 30-second real-time countdown (one in-game day per 6 seconds). Failure to complete within deadline triggers self-destruct sequence.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Time-traveling soldier/warrior defending Earth across human history, wielding era-appropriate weapons (medieval swords, Western firearms, WWII laser tech) against alien invaders.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hard-locked, unforgiving time limit creates persistent urgency rather than leisurely exploration. No continues system—single playthrough sink-or-swim design. Period-specific aesthetics and weapons are cosmetic flavor over mechanical depth; core platforming challenge remains consistent across eras.

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives obtainable through level progression; finite stock
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoints only; failure requires restart from level beginning
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal. Time limit is mandatory and cannot be disabled or adjusted. Weapon acquisition through gift-wrapped packages provides minor resource management element but does not scale difficulty.

progression.upgrade_system: Period-specific weapons acquired via collectible gift-wrapped packages (swords, throwing knives, firearms, grenades, laser weaponry). Weapons are consumable resources rather than permanent upgrades.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Five golden orbs required to power time machine and progress to next era. Four orbs scattered through level environment, fifth obtained by defeating Drakkon Lord boss. Mandatory sequential order: Medieval England (1250 AD) → Western United States (1860 AD) → Caribbean (1650 AD) → France (1943 AD) → Present Day (2999 AD) for final Drakkon King encounter.
replayability_hooks: Time limit encourages speedrunning and optimal route discovery
Weapon placement memorization for efficient runs
High difficulty creates skill-based replay motivation

world_building_techniques: Period-specific environmental aesthetics (Medieval castles, Western frontier towns, Caribbean ports, WWII France)
Weapon and enemy theming tied to historical eras
Time travel framing device connecting disparate settings
Opening exposition: year 2999 alien invasion, historical timeline corruption, self-destruct deadline

boss_design.philosophy: One Drakkon Lord boss per era level; encounter grants final mandatory golden orb. Boss battles serve as progression gates tied to narrative premise (alien elimination per period).

faction_indicators: Drakkon alien invasionist force vs. Time Lord defender protagonist
Historical human populations as implicit allies against invaders
danger_signals: Temporal manipulation attempting to alter past events
Self-destruct mechanism on time machine (stakes escalation)
environmental_storytelling: Medieval England setting with period architecture and enemies
Western frontier aesthetic during pre-Civil War era
WWII France with period-accurate military aesthetic
MB Time Travel Research Centre as modern baseline hub
